title: $length
text: $length Return the length of the specified argument. $length(Argument) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description String String String, field, variable, raw data, or function. Return Values The function $length returns the length of the argument. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The function $length returns the length of the argument in characters. For raw data, you can use the assignment setting $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES in case you want to know the byte size. If String contains frame marker, ruler, or character attribute characters, they are ignored. Example: Using $length The following example shows the results of using $length with variables of type string, numeric, raw data, and floating-point: variables float fnum numeric strLen, num, byteLen string strnum, str raw rawdata endvariables num = -123456 fnum = -1234.56 strnum = "-1,234.56" str = "Hello" rawdata = "{!{#{%{'" strLen = $length(num) ; strLen = 7 strLen = $length(fnum) ; strLen = 8 strLen = $length(strnum) ; strLen = 9 strLen = $length(str) ; strLen = 5 strLen = $length(rawdata) ; strLen = 8, $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES = 0 or is omitted. byteLen= $length(rawdata) ; byteLen= 4, $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES = 1. Copy Related Links $random Generate a random string, raw data of a specified length, or a number between 0 and 1. $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES Specify if function $length returns the length in bytes or characters for arguments with datatype Raw. length (deprecated) Return the number of characters in the specified string.
